query: North Korea nuclear test 2026 - any signs of imminent 7th nuclear test, activity at Punggye-ri test site, recent statements or preparations mid-2026
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the summary of findings on North Korea's potential 7th nuclear test as of mid-2026:

## Key Findings

**No 7th test has occurred.** North Korea's last nuclear test remains September 3, 2017 (its 6th) — nearly 9 years ago.

**Technical readiness vs. imminence:**
- Tunnel No. 3 at Punggye-ri (restored since ~May 2022) is assessed by both U.S. and South Korean intelligence as **physically ready to test on short notice** if Kim Jong Un orders it.
- However, the **most recent public satellite imagery** (Nov 3, 2025) shows **NO signs of an imminent test** — no instruments, cables, power lines, or personnel/vehicle activity.
- Tunnels No. 1, 2, and 4 remain sealed/collapsed since the 2018 disablement.

**Official assessments:**
- SK Defense Intelligence Agency (Nov 5, 2025): test "could be carried out within a short period of time."
- 2025 U.S. DIA: North Korea "postured to conduct a seventh nuclear test at a time of its choosing."
- 38 North (2026): argues a test may not be technically necessary given existing miniaturization, though it could validate the never-tested Hwasan-31 tactical warhead.

**Mid-2026 activity has centered on production, not detonation:**
- June 3-4, 2026: Kim inspected a new weapons-grade fissile material plant, vowing "exponential" expansion.
- June 25-26, 2026: Major **conventional/tactical** weapons tests (rockets, ballistic missiles, howitzers) — **not** a nuclear test.

**Bottom line:** North Korea is postured and able, but as of July 9, 2026 there are no confirmed signs of an imminent 7th nuclear test. The decision is political and rests solely with Kim Jong Un.

Full structured output (key facts, sources, and quantitative data points) has been submitted.

sources.title: Punggye-ri's Tunnel No. 3 ready for use, but no signs of imminent test
sources.snippet: South Korea's Defense Intelligence Agency warned Nov. 5 that North Korea could carry out a nuclear test within a short period; Tunnel No. 3 already physically possible to use. Nov 3, 2025 imagery shows no instruments, cables, power lines, or personnel activity.

sources.title: North Korea's Nuclear Weapons and Missile Programs (CRS, updated May 29, 2026)
sources.snippet: Last nuclear test Sept 3, 2017. IAEA reported tunnel restoration began March 2022. 2025 DIA: North Korea 'is now postured to conduct a seventh nuclear test at a time of its choosing.' April 2026 defense official testified nuclear forces increasingly capable.

sources.title: Recent Activity Observed at Punggye-ri Nuclear Test Facility (Beyond Parallel/CSIS)
sources.snippet: Since 2020, US and South Korea assess North Korea completed all required preparations for a 7th test from Tunnel No. 3. Observed activity is low-level maintenance and does not suggest imminent preparations.

sources.title: Revisiting North Korea's Nuclear Tests (38 North, Feb 2026)
sources.snippet: Argues a 7th test may not be technically necessary given likely existing miniaturization; a new cycle could validate the Hwasan-31 tactical warhead or next-gen MIRV designs.

sources.title: N. Korea appears to be ready for nuclear test once Kim makes decision: defense intelligence
sources.snippet: South Korea's Defense Intelligence Agency assessment (Nov 5, 2025) that North Korea is ready to test pending Kim's decision.

sources.title: North Korea unveils a new plant to produce fuel for nuclear weapons
sources.snippet: Kim Jong Un visited a new facility to produce nuclear bomb fuels June 3, 2026, vowing to expand nuclear forces at an exponential rate.

sources.title: Kim Jong Un inspects new nuclear plant, plans 'exponential' weapons production ramp-up
sources.snippet: Kim inspected a plant making weapons-grade nuclear material June 3, 2026; claimed capacity more than doubled in five years.

sources.title: North Korea conducts major weapons tests; South training 'drone warriors'
sources.snippet: June 25, 2026 conventional/tactical weapons tests (240mm MLRS, tactical ballistic missiles, 155mm howitzer); Kim demanded a deadly and destructive offensive posture.

sources.title: North Korea Is Working on a 'Shortcut' in Punggye-ri Nuclear Test Site #3
sources.snippet: Satellite analysis (Furukawa) caught preparations at the southern entrance to Tunnel No. 3 suggesting possible resumption of testing.

sources.title: Punggye-ri Nuclear Test Site - Wikipedia
sources.snippet: Nuclear tests conducted October 2006, May 2009, February 2013, January 2016, September 2016, and September 2017.
